content.full_text:
Michael Shulman  
111 Bean Creek Road, #80  
Scotts Valley, CA 95066

Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street, Suite 800  
PO Box 807  
Sacramento, CA 95804

Subject: Request for written ruling

Reference: Potential Conflict of Interest for Public Official

I am a Planning Commissioner for the City of Scotts Valley, in Santa Cruz County. There is a major project before us that my personal residence is within 2500 feet of. Our city attorney suggested that a written ruling from the FPPC would be prudent, due to the potential for material affect.

I believe that I have no conflict based on the requirement that any effect on my economic interest will not be distinguishable from the effect on the general public. This belief is based on the following criteria:

1. The population of Scotts Valley is approx. 9,000, with city limits comprising approx. 4 square miles. We thus fit the criteria for a small jurisdiction.
2. Planning Commissioners are required to reside within the jurisdiction.
3. All city elections are at-large.
4. My residence (a condominium) is approx. 1000 feet from the nearest border of the project; there is no foreseeable direct effect of the project on my residence.

Based on the above, your booklet on the Conflict of Interest Law (under the section entitled "Public Generally Rule for Small Jurisdictions") clears me to participate in the decisions on this project. However, since the booklet is not the law, per se, I am requesting a written ruling from you for the record.

If there is any other information you need to complete this request, please call me at (408) 985-2400, ext. 2770. Additional information on the project under discussion can be obtained from the City Planning Department Secretary, Ms. Sandy Adams, at (408) 438-2324.

Your prompt response would be most appreciated, as the next hearing on this matter is scheduled for August 26.

Sincerely,

[signature]
Michael Shulman
